name: Bypass UAC via CMSTP Detected
description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by an auto-elevated instance of the Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer (cmstp.exe). This activity is a strong indicator of an adversary attempting to bypass User Account Control (UAC) to achieve privilege escalation.

prompts.prompt: A critical UAC bypass attempt has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**. The process **{image}** was spawned by a parent process, `cmstp.exe`, which is a known technique for **Bypassing User Account Control** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1548.002**). The command line used for the child process was **{command_line}**, and the event occurred under the user **{user}**. This activity is highly suspicious and requires immediate investigation to determine the nature of the child process and its origin. This could be a malicious actor attempting to execute code with elevated privileges.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The child process created by cmstp.exe.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the child process.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
